title: centaurium tenuiflorum subsp. anglicum
intro: Centaurium tenuiflorum subsp. anglicum, also known as Welsh centaury, is a flowering plant species in the gentian family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe sore throats and reduce fever. The health benefits of Welsh centaury include its ability to act as an expectorant, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and its antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing. Welsh centaury can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, or in dried form as a tincture or capsule, allowing for its medicinal properties to be easily accessed and utilized.
